title: Prompt Injection
description: The system implements defenses against direct and indirect prompt injection attacks that manipulate LLM behavior.

title: Insecure Output Handling
description: LLM outputs are validated and sanitized before being rendered, executed, or passed to downstream systems.

title: Training Data Poisoning
description: Training data integrity is verified and protected against manipulation that could introduce backdoors or biases.

title: Model Denial of Service
description: The system implements rate limiting, input size restrictions, and resource management to prevent DoS attacks on the LLM.

title: Supply Chain Vulnerabilities
description: Third-party models, plugins, and dependencies are vetted for security and integrity before integration.

title: Sensitive Information Disclosure
description: The system prevents the LLM from leaking sensitive data, PII, or proprietary information in its responses.

title: Insecure Plugin Design
description: Plugins and tool integrations enforce least-privilege access and validate all inputs before execution.

title: Excessive Agency
description: The system limits the LLM's ability to take autonomous actions, requiring human approval for high-impact operations.

title: Overreliance
description: The system includes safeguards against users blindly trusting LLM outputs, with confidence indicators and verification prompts.

title: Model Theft
description: The system protects model weights, APIs, and inference endpoints against unauthorized access and extraction.
